For GDPR Privacy Policy applicable to EU Data Subjects, please click here

Kacific Broadband Satellites Ltd., along with our affiliates and subsidiaries (“Kacific”), respects and is committed to the protection of your personal data and your privacy. This Privacy Policy (“Privacy Policy”) explains how we collect, use, and handle your personal data in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act of Singapore, the New Zealand Privacy Act 2020, and other relevant privacy laws in your jurisdiction. As an individual, you have rights under the applicable privacy laws, which may allow or restrict the use of your personal data.

This Privacy Policy applies to personal data in our possession or under our control, including personal data in the possession of organisations we have engaged to collect, use, disclose, or process personal data for our purposes.

column2: >Information We Collect and How We Collect It

column1: We obtain personal data when you choose to provide it, consent to its collection, or where permitted under applicable law, including:
<li>By sending us any inquiries for services
<li>Through third party platforms, including but not limited to:
Types of information we may collect include:
Your personal data is processed by Kacific to give effect to your requested commercial transaction, including but not limited to:
<li>Responding to your inquiries or processing any request for services
<li>Performing accounting, billing reconciliation, auditing, and other internal functions
<li>Protecting against fraud, unlawful activity, claims, or other liabilities

• Allowing certain app features to function, such as accessing your location to identify
    troubleshooting sites and nearby Kacific Authorised Internet Service Providers and
    Distributors

• Providing you with marketing and promotional details (where you have requested
    this).
We may also use your personal data in other ways for which we provide specific notice at the time of collection or where permitted by applicable law.

Our Website uses cookies to track overall site use and to provide you with custom information about our products or services. A cookie is a tiny element of data that a website can send to your browser, which may then be stored on your hard drive so we can recognize you upon your return. You may set your browser to notify you when you receive a cookie. You can also use your browser settings to delete existing cookies and to manage your preference for use of cookies. Cookies may also be required in certain areas of our Website, for example, to authenticate a registered user.
Our sites are not designed to respond to “do not track” signals received from browsers.
We may share your personal data with relevant third parties under the following circumstances:
1. To facilitate a requested offering or transaction, such as with service providers (e.g., lawyers, financial advisors, consultants). 2. If required by law, regulation, or legal process
column2: 3. To law enforcement authorities or government officials
column3: 4. In connection with an investigation of suspected or actual illegal activity.

column1: In the event of a potential or actual sale, merger, acquisition, or reorganisation, your personal data may be transferred to third parties outside your jurisdiction, including New Zealand and Singapore, subject to safeguards under applicable privacy laws.

For individuals in New Zealand, we comply with the Privacy Act 2020, including the Information Privacy Principles (IPPs). If your personal data is transferred overseas, Kacific will ensure appropriate safeguards are in place to protect your privacy.

If you believe we have breached your privacy under New Zealand law, you may contact our Data Protection Officer (DPO) (details below). You may also lodge a complaint with the New Zealand Privacy Commissioner.

We implement administrative, technical, and physical safeguards to protect your personal data against unauthorized access, loss, alteration, or misuse. While we strive to protect your data, no data transmission over the Internet can be guaranteed secure.

column2: >Access to and Correction of Personal Data

column1: You may request access to or correction of your personal data by contacting our DPO at the contact details below. A reasonable fee may apply for access requests.

You may withdraw consent to the collection, use, or disclosure of your personal data by submitting a written request to our DPO. Please note that withdrawal of consent may limit our ability to provide services.

Your personal data may be stored on servers located in Singapore or transferred internationally to our affiliates and service providers. Where required under New Zealand law, we will ensure appropriate safeguards are in place.
We retain personal data only as long as necessary for the purposes stated in this Privacy Policy or to comply with legal obligations.
Our Website may contain links to third-party websites. Kacific is not responsible for these websites’ privacy practices, and we encourage you to review their respective privacy policies.

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Changes will apply to information collected under the previous version unless otherwise stated. By continuing to use our services, you accept any updates to this Privacy Policy.
If you have inquiries or feedback on this Privacy Policy, or wish to make any request regarding your personal data, please contact our Data Protection Officer:
